title: Kiln Monitor
sentToEsp: Sent to ESP32 — alarm fires once when reached.
note_bisque: Moderate ramp · slow steam release at the start
note_glaze: Fast ramp OK above 600°C · hold expected peak
note_crystalline: Very slow ramps · long holds are completely normal
note_raku: Fast ramp and rapid cooling are expected
modal_sub_day: Last activity about a day ago
modal_stored: You have {n} saved datapoints from a previous session.
modal_question: What would you like to do?
confirm_newFiring:
Start a new firing?

· All current datapoints in this browser will be deleted.
· The ESP32 will also reset its saved history.

Export first if you need the data.
confirm_clearChart:
Clear the chart?

· Local cache will be deleted.
· ESP32 datapoints will reload automatically if connected.
confirm_resetAll: Reset all configuration? This will remove all kilns and datapoints from this browser.
confirm_deleteKiln: Delete kiln "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
confirm_relayOn: Turn kiln ON? The relay will close and power the kiln.
confirm_relayOff: Turn kiln OFF? The relay will open and cut kiln power.
contact_lead: Have a question about Páramo, want to collaborate, or found a bug? Reach out — this space is reserved for the right words.
guide_lead: Páramo connects to your kiln's ESP32 via MQTT. Here you'll find setup instructions, connection details, and usage notes. Full documentation coming soon.
guide_step1_desc: Flash the Páramo firmware and configure your WiFi credentials. The ESP32 will publish temperature data to the MQTT broker at regular intervals. — placeholder
guide_step2_desc: Create a free account at HiveMQ Cloud. Copy the host, username and password into the Connection panel in the sidebar. — placeholder
guide_step3_desc: Press Connect. The status pills in the header will turn green when both the MQTT server and the kiln are reachable. Firing data will start flowing. — placeholder
guide_step4_desc: Use the Alarm card to get an alert when a target temperature is reached. Select a firing mode to overlay a reference curve on the chart. — placeholder
guide_step5_desc: When the firing is done, export the chart as JSON for archiving or analysis. Start a new session from the Session card. — placeholder
licence_lead: Páramo is released under the MIT Licence. You are free to use, modify and distribute this software. Credits and attributions below.
relayConfigNote: Device ID = MQTT Client ID set in the Ogemray's MQTT settings.
log_alarmOffDisconnected: Alarm off (not connected — set on ESP once connected)
log_newFiringEsp: New firing started — ESP32 history cleared
log_newFiringLocal: New firing started (local only — ESP32 not connected)
log_relayCmd: Relay command sent — waiting for confirmation…
confirm_resetAll2: Are you absolutely sure? This cannot be undone.
noteSub: Add your personal notes — load, atmosphere, observations. Saved with the firing JSON.
noFiringsMsg: Load your exported JSON files to browse your firing history.
noMatchesMsg: Try adjusting or clearing the filters.
